reforms.description: The Portuguese government initiated a reform in the health sector aimed at encouraging private-sector health care development and introducing greater competition with the public sector. Additionally, plans were announced to create regional networks for skills and employment and to design sectoral programmes for the modernisation of enterprises, partly through increased R&D spending.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging private-sector involvement and modernisation of enterprises is expected to enhance competition and efficiency in the health sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into effect since 1993.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the health sector.
reforms.source_quote: The government also plans to create regional networks for skills and employment and to design and implement sectoral programmes for the modernisation of enterprises, partly through increased R&D spending.

reforms.description: The government announced plans to introduce health education campaigns and epidemiological studies to prevent illness and improve public health outcomes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving public health outcomes through education is expected to enhance overall productivity and reduce healthcare costs.
reforms.status_evidence: The plans have been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the reform is not as transformative as the broader health sector reform.
reforms.source_quote: Proposed measures include health education campaigns in schools and companies as well as epidemiological studies as a means of preventing illness.

reforms.description: The government introduced the Mateus plan, which provides limited and conditional guarantees for medium-term loans to small firms at preferential interest rates, aimed at modernising small and medium-sized enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating access to finance for small firms is expected to enhance their capacity for innovation and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Mateus plan has been in effect since 1997.
reforms.importance_rationale: The plan represents a significant support mechanism for SMEs but is not a structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: To help restructuring, the government in February 1997 introduced limited and conditional guarantees for medium-term loans extended by banks to small firms at preferential interest rates.

reforms.description: The government proposed to stimulate research and development through social sectoral programmes aimed at cutting back red tape.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing bureaucratic barriers is expected to enhance R&D activities and innovation in the public sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposal represents a significant initiative to enhance R&D but is not a major structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: Stimulate research and development social sectoral programmes Implement 1996 Strategic Social Pact and cut back red tape.
